claims.claim: Meta took a ~49% non-voting stake in Scale AI for ~$14.3B in June 2025, valuing it ~$29B; Alexandr Wang left to lead Meta Superintelligence Labs
Scale AI generated ~$870M revenue in 2024 with a ~$1.5B exit run-rate; its ~$2B 2025 target was impaired by the post-Meta customer exodus
Google was Scale's largest customer (~$150M in 2024, ~$200M budgeted 2025) and moved to split after the Meta deal; OpenAI, Microsoft and xAI also pulled back
Surge AI booked >$1B in full-year 2024 revenue, out-earning Scale's $870M, while bootstrapped and profitable with ~110-130 employees
Surge AI sought ~$1B in its first outside round at a valuation negotiated from ~$15B up to ~$25B in mid-2025
Mercor raised $350M Series C at a $10B valuation in Oct 2025 on a ~$450M+ run-rate, up from a $2B mark in Feb 2025
Mercor's reported revenue is gross (total customer spend before contractor payout); contractors receive ~60-70% of top-line, implying a ~30% take rate
Mercor pays >$1.5M/day to 30,000+ experts at ~$85/hr average
Innodata, the only public pure-play, reported FY2025 revenue ~$251.7M at a 39.5% gross margin with ~56-58% of revenue from one Big-Tech customer
Sama labelers on OpenAI's content-moderation contract took home ~$1.32-2/hr while OpenAI was billed ~$12.50/hr, a ~6x markup
Scale/Outlier faces multiple California wage-misclassification lawsuits and a US Department of Labor investigation, with one suit alleging ~$15/hr effective pay below minimum wage
Appen's revenue fell from a US$388.5M FY22 peak to US$234.3M FY24 after Google terminated its ~US$82.8M contract (announced Jan 2024), a ~97% equity drawdown from peak
TELUS Digital's parent TELUS took it private at US$4.50/share (~$539M, +32% over the initial offer), closing Oct 31 2025; Google was its 2nd-largest client at 14.3% of 2024 revenue
Reddit's content-licensing revenue (inside 'Other revenue') stepped up on 2024 Google/OpenAI deals then plateaued: ~$33.2M Q3'24 to ~$33.7M Q1'25, with flat RPO recognition into 2026
Shutterstock's AI-data revenue was $104M in FY23; its 'Data, Distribution and Services' segment grew to $203.3M (21% of revenue) in FY25, with a ~$250M annual target by 2027
Getty Images chose litigation over licensing and largely lost: the UK High Court rejected its core copyright claims against Stability AI on Nov 4 2025
NVIDIA's Nemotron-4 340B used >98% synthetically generated alignment data, but the reward model that filtered it was trained on only ~10K real human preferences

Model collapse from training on recursively generated data is real but conditional: it occurs under indiscriminate replacement, while accumulating synthetic alongside real data avoids it and verification makes synthetic data net-positive
DeepSeek-R1-Zero showed pure RL with verifiable rewards can induce reasoning with no human-labeled reasoning trajectories, peer-reviewed in Nature
Anthropic leaders discussed spending more than $1B on RL environments over the coming year, with rival labs in a similar range
OpenAI funded and had access to the problems in Epoch AI's FrontierMath benchmark, disclosed only at o3's launch
LMArena (Chatbot Arena) raised a ~$100M seed at ~$600M (May 2025, a16z-led), and a peer-reviewed critique found its leaderboard structurally favored a few large providers
Bottom-up frontier-lab spend on external human data, evals and environments is roughly $4-5.5B/yr in 2025, far below vendor-funded 'tens of billions' TAM reports
Meta's flagship TBD Lab uses Surge and Mercor rather than the Scale data Meta part-owns, viewing Scale's data as lower quality
xAI built a ~1,500-person in-house 'AI tutor' annotation team — its largest department — then cut ~500 generalists in Sept 2025 to pivot toward specialists
Turing raised $111M at a $2.2B valuation (Mar 2025) on ~$300M ARR; Invisible Technologies did $134M revenue in 2024 at ~11% EBITDA margin and raised at >$2B in Sept 2025
Public human text stock is projected to be fully utilized roughly 2026-2032 (median ~2028), shifting the marginal data dollar from pretraining scrape to post-training
Ilya Sutskever declared 'we've achieved peak data' at NeurIPS 2024, and Elon Musk agreed AI training data is largely exhausted
